title: Write an Ansible Playbook
description:
## Author a Valid Playbook

Ansible playbooks are YAML: a **list of plays**, each with a `hosts` target and a list of `tasks`.

**Your task:** write a playbook at **`/root/playbook.yml`** that:
1. is a YAML **list** of one or more plays
2. targets the host group **`webservers`** (the `hosts:` key)
3. contains a **`tasks`** list (e.g. one task using the `apt` or `package` module to install nginx)

> 💡 It just needs to be a valid playbook structure — it won't actually run. Validate your YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/playbook.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Playbook skeleton
hints.body: A play is a YAML mapping in a top-level list. It needs hosts and tasks; each task names a module.
hints.command:
printf -- '- hosts: webservers
  become: true
  tasks:
    - name: Install nginx
      apt:
        name: nginx
        state: present
' > /root/playbook.yml

validation.description: Checks that /root/playbook.yml is a valid playbook list targeting 'webservers' with tasks.

commands.description: Valid playbook structure (list of plays with hosts and tasks)
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/playbook.yml')); print('ok' if isinstance(d,list) and len(d)>0 and 'hosts' in d[0] and 'tasks' in d[0] else 'no')"
